title: Target repository primer
summary: Agents MUST use the target repository primer as bounded orientation, MUST read it before expanding target-repository context, and MUST NOT follow its file references without a task-specific need.
instructions: Before expanding target-repository context, every agent MUST read the target repository primer at the harness-relative path `runtime/target-repo-primer.md`. In an embedded installation, Cursor filesystem access MUST use `.pancreator/runtime/target-repo-primer.md`.
The primer is orientation rather than authority. The operator request, target repository `AGENTS.md`, active invocation card, and applicable policies MUST retain precedence over primer content.

Agents MUST NOT open, search, or otherwise follow references from the primer merely because they are listed. An agent MAY inspect a referenced file only when it can state a concrete task-specific reason that the file is needed.

When the primer is missing or marked unbuilt, non-librarian agents MUST stop before substantive target-repository work and direct the operator to run `/pan-build-docs`. The librarian MAY proceed to create the missing primer and MUST read the previous primer first when it exists.

The librarian MUST inspect representative target code, target `AGENTS.md`, `README.md`, other relevant documentation, setup/build/installation/test scripts, package or project manifests, and bounded Git history when Git metadata is available.

The librarian MUST write runtime/repository-checks.json from verified target-repository commands, MUST preserve documented distinctions among fast/default, secondary slow/integration, and complete suites, MUST use explicit repository-declared toolchain entrypoints when available, and MUST leave uncertain profiles empty rather than infer a language or package manager.

The librarian MUST produce a concise primer containing a prose summary, administrative commands, a Mermaid architecture diagram, high-level project structure with key paths, public interfaces, and gotchas or explicit `None identified` statements.

The librarian MUST write only the declared primer path and runtime/repository-checks.json, MUST NOT alter target source or workflow state, MUST validate the repository-check configuration, and MUST run the primer's resolved policy-bound validator under VALID-001 before presenting the documentation bundle as complete.
